name: Temporal Decay Query Primitive (TDQP)
overview: TDQP evaluates confidence decay at read time using configurable decay functions (exponential, linear, step). Every ConfidenceEnvelope carries decay parameters, and the primitive computes current confidence in 6.67ns per evaluation (150M evals/sec). This makes temporal awareness effectively free at query time.

howItWorks.title: Decay Configuration
howItWorks.description: Each ConfidenceEnvelope includes a decay function type (exponential, linear, step), half-life duration, and floor value. These are set at write time and travel with the data through B-epsilon buffers.

howItWorks.title: Read-Time Evaluation
howItWorks.description: When a value is read, TDQP computes elapsed time since the envelope's creation timestamp and applies the configured decay function. The result is the current effective confidence.

howItWorks.title: Floor Enforcement
howItWorks.description: Confidence never drops below the configured floor value (typically 0.1). This prevents old-but-valid data from being treated as zero-confidence. The floor is part of the envelope, not a global setting.

howItWorks.title: Batch Optimization
howItWorks.description: During range scans, TDQP evaluates decay for all matching envelopes in a tight loop. The 6.67ns per-evaluation cost means scanning 1M entries adds only ~6.7ms of decay overhead.

comparisons.source: Function call + datetime parsing per read

comparisons.narrative: No production database implements temporal confidence decay as a storage primitive. Systems that need time-aware confidence must implement it in application code, paying function-call overhead per read and risking inconsistency between decay logic versions across services.

competitors.approach: Application-layer functions or materialized views with scheduled refresh
competitors.limitation: Stale between refreshes; per-row function cost on live queries; no configurable decay curves
Binary alive/dead — no gradual confidence reduction; data is deleted, not decayed
Coarse time-based deletion, not per-value confidence decay
Must compute decay in Cypher or application code per traversal
